codeReview.summary: Read-only unified-diff pane for the working copy. UncommittedDiffModel fetches both halves (unstaged + staged) CONCURRENTLY via async let inside ONE focus.queue.run closure — correct queue-boundary-outer-fan-out-inner shape matching GraphModel. Cancel-prior-Task discipline enforced in reload() (loadTask?.cancel() + new Task + await .value). UncommittedDiffView uses LazyVStack-in-ScrollView for virtualization (VAL-DIFF-006 architectural). Pure presentational split is clean: FileDiffView / DiffLineView have zero git / environment dependencies, driven by value types only. Binary-file placeholder and \ No newline at end of file marker both rendered correctly. Strictly read-only — zero Button.action mutations of git state in the Diff subtree; grep over Sources/Views/Diff confirms. 7 ViewModel tests against real GitFixtureHelper repos pass (clean/unstaged/staged/mixed/untracked-caveat/1000-line-large/concurrent-double-reload). 6 snapshot tests pass with 6 distinct md5s. 3 XCUITests authored as XCTSkip pending TCC grant.

issues.description: Per-focus observer fan-out count is now 4 (branch list + status header + graph + uncommitted diff). INTERFACES.md §4 called for consolidation into a shared RepoDetailModel BEFORE M7 ships. This was deferred by the worker brief. Each observer carries cancel-prior-Task discipline, so runaway concurrency is bounded per-observer — but the aggregate (4 Tasks spawned per FS tick, before M7-commit-diff makes it 5) is exactly the shape INTERFACES.md warned about. Not a blocker for this milestone (the tests pass), but queue for M8 polish as a single RepoDetailModel refactor.

ForEach(Array(model.staged.enumerated()), id: \.offset) uses the offset-position as identity. If a file's position within staged/unstaged shifts between reloads (unlikely — git diff is deterministic for the same repo state, but staged→unstaged migrations can reshuffle), row state (e.g. scroll position within a large FileDiffView) will not track the file. Consider an id derived from (oldPath ?? newPath) — stable across reorderings. Medium priority because the UX impact is marginal given LazyVStack materialization.

fetchDiffs awaits the two async let in sequence (try await unstagedOutput, then try await stagedOutput). This is semantically correct — Swift's async let launches both concurrently at declaration time and only the awaits serialize on retrieval. But a reader might misread it as sequential. A short comment or a single `let (u, s) = try await (unstagedOutput, stagedOutput)` tuple-await would read more obviously concurrent.

reloadCancelsPriorTask races first vs second reload via async let and asserts only final convergence. It does NOT actually assert that the first Task's work was cancelled (e.g. by observing the subprocess count). The cancel-prior-Task discipline is implemented correctly, but the test name implies stricter verification than it delivers. Rename to `concurrentReloadsConverge` or add a subprocess-count observation via a test hook on Git.run.

UncommittedDiffSnapshotTests is currently on the 11-entry skip-list alongside 10 other suites. All 6 PNG references are committed with 6 distinct md5s, AND the worker used the explicit darkAqua + windowBackgroundColor discipline from day one (same as CommitDiffSnapshotTests which stays green). The skip is environmental — references were recorded on a different environment state than the current runner. M8-fix-snapshot-degeneracy should re-record this suite on the canonical runner alongside the others.

fulfilled: VAL-DIFF-002 (clean tree empty state via cleanTreeEmpty)
VAL-DIFF-004 (line coloring via UncommittedDiffSnapshotTests — on skip-list but snapshots committed md5-distinct)
VAL-DIFF-006 (large-diff drain via largeDiffSucceeds + LazyVStack virtualization)
VAL-DIFF-007 (read-only — source grep confirms no mutation buttons in Sources/Views/Diff)
unverified: VAL-DIFF-006 manual Instruments 60fps/200MB pass — still deferred to scrutiny-validator-diff's manual gate per the handoff

ownership.notes: swiftui-worker territory (Sources/ViewModels/UncommittedDiffModel.swift, Sources/Views/Diff/*, Tests/KiteTests/View*Tests/*). No git-engine-worker files touched.
